description: As Skye and Ward enter the Triskelion, she witnesses Hydra agents violently detaining an Inhuman, painfully underscoring the dystopian reality they inhabit. Ward brushes off her concern, but Skye is deeply unsettled as she realizes the full extent of Hydra's oppression. She pretends to check her email, using the opportunity to search for her friends, only to discover that Lincoln and Jemma Simmons are listed as dead. Melinda May confronts Skye, leading to a tense and disjointed interaction as Skye desperately tries to break through to her old ally.

transcript.line: Yeah, some mornings, the Triskelion looks so beautiful.
Come on. We're gonna be late.
You can't do this!
Son of a...
Hey!
They can handle it on their own. Come on.
What are you doing? You're on another planet this morning. Look, I get it. You put yourself out there last night, and I let you down. I'm sorry.
What are you apologizing for?
It's been a few years. It makes sense to take this step. I would love to live with you. It's just... it's not the right time.
I asked you to move in? I, um... I mean, I did. And you're right, it's a bad idea.
I just need some time, some space. Skye, there are things about me that you wouldn't like if you knew.
Trust me. I know.
Skye.
Ward.
It's that way.
Kill me already.
Hey, you ever finish the Saunders report? Of course you did.
I'm gonna check my e-mail.
Why? Anyone who's e-mailing you is gonna be in the briefing.
Yeah, that's why I want to get caught up.
Okay.
“Died during testing?” What did they do? No, no, no, no, no. No, no, no, no. It can't be.
Can't be what?
May. You're here. Are you okay? Are you okay? I came looking for you.
Get started with the briefing.
No, no, no, May, it's me. I... I got into the Framework.
I don't care what you've hacked.
May, look at me. It's me... it's D...
Do I have to get you drug-tested? Get in the briefing.
Sorry. I'll come now.
